latest.title: Squirrels memorize every nut they bury
latest.script_key: this squirrel just walked past its own stash without grabbing it look at those pauses it s literally sniffing the bark like a map that s why squirrels remember thousands of buried spots not just by memory but by using landmarks did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Dogs build a map with smell
latest.script_key: dogs build a map with smell watch the nose before the feet because every sniff samples layers of scent dogs can learn who passed where they moved and what changed since the last check the ground is basically a news feed what should they sniff next

latest.title: Dolphins can call each other with signature whistles
latest.script_key: dolphins can call each other with signature whistles watch the group spacing because dolphins use sound to keep track of individuals in open water a signature whistle works like a personal label helping the pod reconnect when visibility drops ocean social life is loud which sound should we decode next

latest.title: Deer aim their ears like sound radar
latest.script_key: deer can aim each ear toward danger watch the ears first because deer can turn them to sample sound from different directions that quick listening check helps them decide whether to freeze flee or keep feeding one quiet second tells the whole body what to do would you freeze

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Golden retrievers tilt heads for a real reason
latest.script_key: watch their heads tilt every time you speak they re not just cute that tilt scoops up sound waves like satellite dishes the left ear picks up high pitch faster so they cock right to balance the delay that s why they seem to lean in did you know humans do this too when we can t hear

latest.title: Whales keep the ocean dark green
latest.script_key: watch the whale s tail that dark green isn t just water it s floating life whales poop near the surface and that waste fertilizes plankton blooms because plankton stores carbon every whale is a tiny climate machine did you learn they just swim around

latest.title: How dogs cool down without sweating like us in the first second
latest.script_key: dogs cool themselves through panting paws and posture watch the first second the body pauses then the mouth and paws gives the clue in this clip the recovery angle is golden retriever sitting on the clue is not the cute part it is the timing of the movement that visible cue is the whole story so the short opens on the dog instead of stretching the setup that makes the short feel observed not copied
